what_it_does: Specifies a derivation plan for dna/voice as an addressable voice standard covering card-copy registers and the RHM spoken register.
observations: Defines two surface classes: written card fields and spoken RHM prose.
Assigns card fields to registers: name, meaning/question, label, implication, and is.
Defines spoken-register constraints: second-person address, multi-turn structure, phone-aware brevity, human translation of machine references, and context-based answers.
Identifies raw material for extraction: PANEL_BRIEFING, territory_prose framing, dna/voice.json, canon/voice work, actual elegant copy, and recollection's 7 cards.
Describes register handles resolved at fill-time and bound by composition and fork.
Gives a sequence from DNA derivation through recollection, fork, projection verification, and Tim reaction.
standouts: The file frames the voice standard as extracted from existing language rather than invented.
It treats spoken RHM prose as a distinct register instead of a card field.
It emphasizes addressable register handles so multiple surfaces resolve through one standard.
conventions: Uses emphatic uppercase phrases to mark non-negotiable method and process constraints.
Uses field-to-register mapping language for card copy.
Uses ownership/status metadata at the top.
Uses named component/channel references and bracketed relation syntax.
Keeps the document as a prose spec rather than schema or executable configuration.
concerns: Several external artifacts and owners are named but not defined inside the file.
The register-handle scheme is described as needed but not specified in concrete shape here.
Some terms such as RHM, fork, projection, and cosine-degrade assume surrounding project context.
notes: This file is a local spec for deriving and resolving a shared voice standard across card copy and spoken operator prose.
questions: What exact structure will the register-handle scheme use?
What criteria does projection use to verify that text reads as Tim's voice?
What are the seven recollection cards referenced as the card-register test corpus?
What does the cosine-degrade machine-note refer to in this context?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it gathers the as-is material and specifies how dna/voice should be derived, addressed, and adopted by related surfaces.
apparent_themes: single standard across multiple prose surfaces
intention_signals: It seems intended to prevent voice drift by defining a derived, addressable language standard that card copy and spoken RHM prose can both resolve through.
novelty: The distinct element is treating voice as resolvable register handles spanning both written card fields and spoken multi-turn operator dialogue.
contribution: Contributes a file-local voice-register ledger, source-corpus list, spoken-register constraints, and a sequencing model for deriving and applying dna/voice across named surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: A prose spec for deriving dna/voice from existing language and resolving written card fields plus spoken RHM prose through addressable register handles.
intention_for_embedding: This was written to guide extraction, calibration, binding, and verification of a shared voice standard without inventing a new voice.
